_comment: Settings for the prompt-auto-enhance plugin. Everything is ON by default, so a fresh install gives you the full prompt-improver. To change anything: copy this file to <your-project>/.claude/enhance-settings.json (per-project) OR ~/.claude/enhance-settings.json (global, applies to ALL projects), or run /enhance-config. Changes take effect on your NEXT message. The _help section explains every setting in plain English.

_help.enabled: Master switch for the whole prompt-improver. false = turn everything off.
_help.when_to_run: automatic = improve every real prompt; ask_first = only improve when you reply 'enhance'; off = never improve (plugin stays installed).
_help.after_improving: run_immediately = improve the prompt and answer in one go; let_me_review_first = improve the prompt, show it, then STOP and wait for you to approve/edit/run it.
_help.enhance_slash_commands: false (default) = NEVER improve a /command or saved custom prompt (whether you made it or Anthropic provided it) — run it exactly as-is, any size. true = treat them like normal prompts.
_help.when_to_enhance.skip_short_prompts: Don't improve tiny prompts. count_by words or characters; minimum = the cutoff below which a prompt is skipped.
_help.when_to_enhance.skip_these_phrases: Exact replies that are just continuations (yes/ok/thanks...) and shouldn't be improved.
_help.when_to_enhance.skip_phrases_starting_wi: Short prompts that begin with these (now do.../also...) are follow-ups, not new tasks — skip them.
_help.when_to_enhance.skip_if_just_a_question: true = if you're only ASKING a fact (not asking for a task), just answer — don't rewrite the question.
_help.when_to_enhance.skip_tip_for_simple_task: true = don't add the 'better phrasing' suggestion for obvious tasks (fix a typo, what-does-this-do).
_help.when_to_enhance.also_check_when_short_pr: true = even a very short prompt gets the full treatment if it triggers a lot of work.
_help.when_to_enhance.min_response_size_to_che: Only treat an answer as 'substantial' (worth quality-checking) once it passes this many characters.
_help.display.show_the_process: true = show you the improvement steps; false = improve silently and just answer.
_help.display.how_much_to_show: every_time = always show the full steps; scale_to_prompt_quality = great prompt -> short note, okay -> compact, weak -> full; only_for_weak_prompts = full steps only for weak prompts, else a one-liner. A prompt is 'weak' when it scores below weak_prompt_score_below.
_help.display.weak_prompt_score_below: The score (out of 10) under which a prompt counts as 'weak'.
_help.display.show_step_log_only_for_multipart: true = only show the step-by-step log when your request has several distinct parts.
_help.display.show: Checkboxes for WHICH parts you see. Any combination on/off (presets: /enhance-config render all | render none).
_help.improving.dont_rewrite_if_prompt_is_alre: Leave an already-good prompt alone. excellent = only skip rewriting truly excellent prompts; good_or_better = also skip good ones; never = always rewrite.
_help.improving.always_add_a_role: true = always add an 'Act as ...' role to the prompt, regardless of its score.
_help.scoring_criteria: The rubric used to score a prompt. Each criterion has a weight; the weights must add up to 1.0.
_help.ask_clarifying_questions: Whether to ask you a question when the request is ambiguous. method = which skill it uses (grill-me asks one question at a time). ask_until_confidence = how sure it must be before proceeding; max_questions 0 = no limit.
_help.make_sure_steps_were_shown: The plugin's self-check that it actually showed its work. strict = stop and redo if a step is missing; relaxed = just keep a note; off = don't check.
_help.keep_a_quiet_log: true = quietly record whenever a step got skipped, so you can review later.
_help.background_research: How much surrounding info (your project files, settings, history) to read before answering. light / normal / deep.
